subject: Human Resources
title: Wrongful Termination
description: A short lesson to help you apply Wrongful Termination.

hook.text: An employee is fired the day after filing a safety complaint. Coincidence? The law says: prove it wasn't retaliation.

buildup.text: Wrongful termination occurs when firing violates law—discrimination, retaliation, breach of contract, or public policy.

discovery.text: Even in at-will states, employers cannot fire for illegal reasons—protected class, whistleblowing, exercising legal rights.

twist.text: Timing is often the strongest evidence.

climax.text: Documentation is the employer's best defense—consistent records showing legitimate, non-retaliatory reasons for the decision.

punchline.text: You can fire for any reason—except the wrong one.

quiz.question: What makes timing important in wrongful termination cases?
options: Firing shortly after protected activity suggests retaliation
